title: diversifier_index stored as decimal TEXT, not INTEGER, in accounts table
description: db.rs:297-303 — accounts.diversifier_index is TEXT (decimal u128), not INTEGER. This is correct: SQLite INTEGER is signed 64-bit; u128::MAX is ~3.4e38, far above i64::MAX ~9.2e18. A u128 stored as INTEGER would silently truncate to a negative value. The comment explicitly documents why and says DO NOT change to INTEGER. The diversifier_index_roundtrip test writes u128::MAX to catch this regression. Future contributors will notice the TEXT column and 'simplify' it to INTEGER — the comment and test are both needed to defend this decision.
